name: Trigonometry & Height and Distances Practice Test

questions.question: A tower stands vertically on the ground. From a point on the ground 50 m away from the base, the angle of elevation of the top is 30°. Find height. (tan30°=1/√3)
From a cliff 200 m high, the angle of depression to a ship is 30°. How far is the ship from the base? (tan30°=1/√3)
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point is 45°. If the height of the tower is 100 m, find the distance of the point from the base.
An observer 1.5 m tall looks at the top of a building at an angle of 60°. If his eyes are 20 m from the building, find the height of the building. (tan60°=√3)
From the top of a building 60 m high, the angles of depression of two points A and B on the ground are 30° and 60°. How far apart are A and B? (tan30°=1/√3, tan60°=√3)
The shadow of a vertical pole is 1/√3 times its height. Find the angle of elevation of the sun.
Two poles of heights 6 m and 11 m stand on a plane ground. If the distance between feet is 12 m, find distance between tops.
questions.explanation: Height diff=5 m. Horizontal=12 m. Distance=√(25+144)=√169=13 m.

questions.question: A ladder 10 m long rests against a wall making angle 60° with the ground. Height of the ladder from the ground is:
An aeroplane flying horizontally at 1 km height observes a city at 30° depression. After flying for 10 sec, the depression is 60°. Speed of aeroplane is:
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from two points P and Q at distances 4 m and 9 m are complementary. The height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: tanα×tanβ=1 for complementary angles. h/4 × h/9=1. h²=36. h=6 m.
